# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains a self-reported engineering experiment about using an orchestrator agent with delegated sub-agents for a long-running coding task, including workflow description, task scope, cost and token figures, human intervention points, file-change counts, testing outcomes, and claimed end-state functionality.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The workflow used a main dialogue as an orchestrator agent that explicitly delegated individual tasks to sub-agents, then verified and integrated their results.

## Source Excerpt

"Approach: the main dialogue (or session in terms of OpenCOde) is the an orchestrator agent; you explicitly ask it to delegate individual tasks to sub-agents ... verify them, and integrate the results."

## Note

This is a direct description of the workflow the author says was used.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: claim-support
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The stated reason for delegating to sub-agents was to avoid hitting the model context window limit.

## Source Excerpt

"Why? Cause we don't want to hit the context window limit of the model."

## Note

This source states the intended rationale for the delegation pattern.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The task in the experiment was to rewrite a previously vibe-coded LiteLLM provider that cascades requests across several LLMs before returning a final response.

## Source Excerpt

"Task: rewrite a previously vibe-coded provider for litellm which implements a cascade of requests to several LLMs ... before returning a final response."

## Note

The source identifies the implementation target and intended behavior.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The reported agent work time was about 4 hours.

## Source Excerpt

"Results:
About 4 hours of pure agent work time"

## Note

This is a self-reported duration from the author.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The orchestrator session was reported as costing $4.13 and reaching 157k tokens of dialogue length by the end of the task.

## Source Excerpt

"Orchestrator session — $4.13, 157k tokens of dialogue length by the end of the task"

## Note

These figures are reported by the author for the main session only.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric

---

```

---

```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h

---
The author reports that only 5 tests were written and that all of them were green.

```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: result
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The author claims the app works, with the provider executing multiple LLM queries and the Streamlit dashboard showing recorded traces.

## Source Excerpt

"The app works — the provider executes multiple llm queries aggregating the final respond, the Streamlit dashboard shows the recorded traces."

## Note

This is a self-reported functional outcome; the same source also reports limited test count, so the operational claim should remain attached to that limitation.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For about 4 hours of agent time, the author reports about half an hour of human effort and about 10 user messages.

## Source Excerpt

"For these ~4 hours of agent time, it took about half an hour of human effort and ~10 user messages."

## Note

This is a self-reported measure of human involvement.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: claim-support
citation: Saplin, DEV Community, Jan 22
source_url: https://dev.to/maximsaplin/long-horizon-agents-opencode-gpt-52-codex-experiment-1f4h
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The author states that the orchestrator/sub-agent approach fit about 2 million tokens of work into a 157K-token main thread and says GPT-5.2 Codex still had room within a 400K context window.

## Source Excerpt

"The orchestrator/subagents approach has effectively allowed to fit in 2 million tokens worth of work into 157K token long main thread with the orchestrator - there's still room given that GPT-5.2 Codex has a 400K context window."

## Note

This is the source's explicit claim about how the orchestration pattern affected main-thread token usage.
